{{Quote|What you have is here and now—yesterday doesn't matter and tomorrow doesn't exist.|Hondo Karr to Anj Dahl|<ref name="Legacy 41" />}}
'''Hondo Karr''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] who served next to [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Chernan Ordo]] in the [[Battle of Botajef]]. During the battle he was betrayed and, in order to survive and expose the traitor, put on the [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|armor]] of a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] and was conscripted into the [[Joker Squad]], a unit in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Army]] of the [[Fel Empire]]. Ten years later, during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Karr deserted the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|new Empire]] at the [[Battle of Borosk (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Borosk]] and found his way into the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]]'s famed [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], where he fought for some time, until a chance arose for him to reunite with his former wife and return to the Mandalorian ways, deciding to hunt down the one who betrayed him and bring him to justice.

==Biography==
===Mandalorian===
In [[127 ABY]] Hondo Karr served next to ''Mand'alor'' Chernan Ordo in the [[Battle of Botajef]]. During the battle, another Mandalorian by the name of [[Yaga Auchs]], betrayed his comrades, which led to the ''Mand'alor's'' death, and left Karr to die. Found by [[Vevec]], father of his wife [[Tes Vevec|Tes]], Karr decided to get off-world and expose Auchs's treachery. He removed his [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|''beskar'gam'']] and put on the armor of a dead [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]]. Soon, Vevec was killed by advancing troopers and Karr, posing as the last soldier of his unit, was accepted in the [[407th Stormtrooper Division]].

===Imperial Stormtrooper===
{{Quote|Hondo Karr is…well…Hondo Karr. Some say he's ex-Mandalorian. Hondo…doesn't say much.|Jes Gistang|<ref name="Legacy (2006) 4" />}}
During the [[Battle of Borosk (137 ABY)|Battle of Borosk]], Karr was shocked at the notion of being ordered to kill fellow stormtroopers. Despite his feelings, he continued to follow his orders. But the final straw came after the battle was over. [[Darth Maleval]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] overseeing the battle, put Lieutenant [[Gil Cassel]], Karr's commanding officer, through a cruel loyalty test: he was to kill [[Jared Cassel|his own brother]], or be executed as a traitor himself. Gil refused, and the Sith Lord executed both men. Karr, enraged at this, attacked Maleval with a [[Vibroknife/Legends|vibroknife]]. But Maleval simply knocked Karr out of the way. Saying that avenging a traitor made him a traitor, Maleval attacked Karr with his electro-whip. However, the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] Sith Lord was shot in the back by [[Anson Trask]], a fellow member of the 407th Division. Karr complemented Trask on his shooting, and left. The official report by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Ran Harkas]] stated that Karr had been killed in the battle.

===Fighting for the Alliance===
{{Quote|What's his name? The ex-stormie.|Anj Dahl|<ref name="Legacy (2006) 20">[[Legacy (2006) 20|''Legacy'' (2006) 20]]</ref>}}
[[File:HondoKarr-LECG.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Hondo Karr in Rogue uniform]]
Karr left the Empire and joined [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]] and the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]]. He was made a member of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], despite his past. Karr later earned the respect of [[Andurgo]], a fellow Rogue pilot, when he took the blame for a knife fight that they had gotten into.

[[File:Hondo_Karr_Mando.jpg|thumb|Hondo Karr, back in his Mandalorian ''beskar'gam''.]]
At the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Second Imperial Civil War)|Battle of Mon Calamari]], he led a task force of Alliance troops disguised as stormtroopers as part of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]]'s plan to hijack the [[Imperious-class Star Destroyer|Advanced Star Destroyer]] [[Alliance (Imperious-class)|''Imperious'']]. He later became involved on his risky [[Mission to Napdu|mission]] to smuggle a group of [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] refugees from [[Napdu]]. While the mission went successfully, he was confronted by his former wife [[Tes Vevec]], who believed Karr had killed her father and was seeking vengeance. However, Karr was able to disarm her and convince her to believe his true story. Reunited with his wife, Karr left Rogue Squadron, once again donned a suit of Mandalorian armor which he repainted black and gold, the traditional Mandalorian colors for "justice" and "vengeance", and swore to track down Yaga Auchs, who'd declared himself the new ''Mand'alor''.

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|A warrior's more than his armor, Vevec.|Hondo Karr|<ref name="Legacy 41" />}}
A Mandalorian and ex-stormtrooper, Karr had a silent, intense, yet laid back and humorous personality and a fondness for vibroknives. His experiences on the battlefield, and the deaths of individuals such as Vevec and Jes Gistang, had led him to avoid forming attachments to his comrades-in-arms. Nevertheless he kept a personal code of loyalty to those that served alongside him, including his superiors, as well as a dislike for unnecessary cruelty, an attitude that prompted him to attack Darth Maleval when the Sith Lord executed the Cassel brothers.

==Behind the scenes==
A figure of Hondo Karr was created by Entertainment Earth and unveiled at Comic Con International 2008 as part of the [[Joker Squad]] action figure 6-pack.
